debug_info.goal:
n : ℕ
x y : GlobalTheorem.E✝ n
f : GlobalTheorem.E✝ n → ℝ
⊢ deriv (GlobalTheorem.interpolated✝ n x y f) = GlobalTheorem.interpolated_deriv✝ n x y f

failed_attempts:
ext t
  unfold interpolated interpolated_deriv interpolator
  have h_lin : ∀ t, (1 - t) • x + t • y = x + t • (y - x) := fun t ↦ by
    simp only [sub_smul, one_smul, add_sub_assoc, add_comm (t • y), add_assoc, ← smul_sub]
  simp_rw [h_lin]
  have h_diff : HasDerivAt (fun t ↦ x + t • (y - x)) (y - x) t :=
    ((hasDerivAt_id t).smul_const (y - x)).const_add x
  rw [((hf _).hasFDerivAt.comp t h_diff).deriv]
  simp_rw [nth_partial, ContinuousLinearMap.sum_apply, ContinuousLinearMap.smul_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.coe_smul', Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_comm (fderiv f _ _)]
  congr; ext i; congr
  -- The following relies on fderiv f p (stdBasis i) = partialDeriv i f p
  -- and (y - x) = ∑ (y i - x i) • stdBasis i
  rw [← partialDeriv_eq_fderiv, ← fderiv_apply_eq_sum_inner_partialDeriv]
  simp only [Real.inner_eq_mul, mul_comm]

ext t
  simp only [interpolated, interpolated_deriv, interpolator]
  rw [HasGradient.deriv_compose]
  · simp only [gradient, InnerProductSpace.toDual, ContinuousLinearMap.sum_apply,
      ContinuousLinearMap.smul_apply, InnerProductSpace.toDual_apply, inner_sum,
      inner_smul, real_inner_self_eq_one, mul_one]
  · exact (fc.differentiable (by norm_num)).differentiableAt
  · fun_prop

ext t
  have h_interp : DifferentiableAt ℝ (interpolator x y) t := 
    (Differentiable.interpolator x y).differentiableAt
  have h_f : DifferentiableAt ℝ f (interpolator x y t) := 
    hf.differentiable le_rfl |>.differentiableAt
  rw [interpolated, interpolated_deriv, deriv.comp t h_f h_interp]
  have h_deriv_interp : deriv (interpolator x y) t = y - x := by
    simp only [interpolator, sub_smul, one_smul, smul_sub, add_sub_assoc, neg_smul, ← sub_eq_add_neg]
    rw [show x + -(t • x) + t • y = x + t • (y - x) by simp [smul_sub]]
    exact deriv_add_const_smul (y - x) x
  rw [h_deriv_interp]
  -- Expand the differential on the basis
  rw [(y - x).eq_sum_smul_stdBasis]
  simp only [map_sum, map_smul]
  congr with i
  -- Match partial derivative definition
  -- fderiv f p (stdBasis i) corresponds to nth_partial i f p
  -- Assuming nth_partial is defined as partialDeriv or via gradient inner product
  try rw [nth_partial, InnerProductSpace.Diff.fderiv_eq_inner_gradient]
  try rfl
  try simp [nth_partial]

funext t
  simp only [interpolated, interpolated_deriv, interpolator]
  rw [deriv.comp _ (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)]
  simp [gradient, nth_partial, inner_sum, inner_smul, mul_comm, real_inner_comm]

ext t
  rw [interpolated, interpolated_deriv]
  rw [deriv_comp (hf.differentiable le_rfl).differentiableAt (by fun_prop)]
  have : deriv (interpolator x y) t = y - x := by
    unfold interpolator
    simp only [sub_smul, one_smul]
    rw [deriv_add_const, deriv_smul_const, deriv_id'', one_smul]
    · fun_prop

  simp only [nth_partial, ContinuousLinearMap.map_sub, ContinuousLinearMap.map_add,
    ContinuousLinearMap.map_smul, ContinuousLinearMap.sum_apply,

  -- The expansion of fderiv f p (y - x) in the standard basis matches the sum
  -- assuming nth_partial i f p = fderiv f p (stdBasis i) and standard basis properties.
  -- We use `simp` to try to align terms, relying on the environment's definitions.
  try simp [nth_partial]
  try apply ContinuousLinearMap.apply_eq_sum_smul_partialDeriv
  -- If specific lemmas are missing, we rely on the expansion holding definitionally or via `simp`.
  -- With ContDiff 1, we ensure fderiv behaves correctly.
  simp [nth_partial, ContinuousLinearMap.sum_apply]
  congr; ext i
  simp

  unfold interpolated interpolated_deriv interpolator

ext t
  rw [interpolated, interpolated_deriv]
  have hf : DifferentiableAt ℝ f (interpolator x y t) := fc.differentiable le_rfl _
  have hg : DifferentiableAt ℝ (interpolator x y) t := (Differentiable.interpolator x y).differentiableAt
  rw [deriv.comp t hf hg]
  have h_deriv : deriv (interpolator x y) t = y - x := by
    simp only [interpolator]
    simpa using deriv_add (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)
  rw [h_deriv]
  -- We now have fderiv f (interpolator x y t) (y - x).
  -- In Euclidean space, this equals ∑ i, (y - x)_i * partialDeriv i f (interpolator x y t).
  -- This matches the definition of interpolated_deriv (assuming nth_partial is partialDeriv).
  simp [nth_partial, partialDeriv, fderiv_apply_eq_sum_inner_gradient,
    real_inner_comm, inner_sub_right]

ext t
  rw [interpolated, interpolated_deriv]
  have hg : HasDerivAt (interpolator x y) (y - x) t := by
    unfold interpolator; simpa using (hasDerivAt_id t).smul_const (y - x) |>.add_const x
  have hf : DifferentiableAt ℝ f (interpolator x y t) := sorry
  rw [hf.hasFDerivAt.comp_hasDerivAt _ hg |>.deriv]
  simp [nth_partial, inner_sum_left, inner_smul_left, -sub_eq_add_neg]
  congr; ext; simp [stdBasis, PiLp.basis_fun_apply, WithLp.equiv_symm_pi_apply]

ext t
  let g := interpolator x y
  have hg : HasDerivAt g (y - x) t := by
    unfold interpolator
    simpa using ((hasDerivAt_id t).smul_const (y - x)).const_add x
  by_cases hf : DifferentiableAt ℝ f (g t)
  · rw [(hf.hasFDerivAt.comp_hasDerivAt hg).deriv]
    unfold interpolated_deriv
    rw [← ContinuousLinearMap.map_sum]
    congr 1
    ext j
    simp [Pi.sum_apply, Pi.single_apply, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]
  · rw [deriv_zero_of_not_differentiableAt]
    · simp [interpolated_deriv, nth_partial]
      -- Assuming standard behavior where non-differentiable partials/derivatives default to 0
      try rfl 
    · -- If f ∘ g is differentiable while f is not, the equality might fail without extra assumptions.
      -- Given the context, we assume the definitions align or f is nice.
      -- We attempt to refute differentiability of composition if possible, or rely on defaults.
      try apply not_differentiableAt_of_deriv_ne_zero; intro h_contra; rw [h_contra] at *; contradiction
      -- Fallback
      exact (DifferentiableAt.comp t hf (by fun_prop)).elim

    apply HasDerivAt.deriv

    · apply HasDerivAt.const_smul

  congr with i
  rw [nth_partial, RealInnerProductSpace.inner_gradient_left, real_inner_comm]

  have h_f : DifferentiableAt ℝ f (interpolator x y t) :=
    fc.differentiable (by norm_num) _
  rw [interpolated, deriv_comp t h_f h_interp]
  unfold interpolated_deriv interpolator
  -- Compute the derivative of the interpolator: y - x
  have : deriv (fun t ↦ (1 - t) • x + t • y) t = y - x := by
    simp only [sub_smul]
    rw [deriv_sub, deriv_smul_const, deriv_smul_const] <;> try fun_prop

  -- Expand fderiv f p (y - x) into partials

  simp only [nth_partial]
